data.title: Children's Place Inc reports results for the quarter ended January 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Children's Place Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of 75 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended January 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $-7.38. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 26 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 16 cents to 35 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 10.2% to $408.56 million from a year ago; analysts expected $380.79 million.

  • Children's Place Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 62 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $7.99 million.

  • Children's Place Inc shares had fallen by 19.0% this quarter and lost 32.3% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 8.5% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is no "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the apparel & accessories retailers peer group is also "hold"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Children's Place Inc is $8.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 11 at 09:54 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Beam Global Reports Full Year 2024 Operating Results
data.description:
Beam Global, a leading provider of innovative and sustainable infrastructure solutions for the electrification of transportation, smart cities, and energy security, has released its operating results for the year ended December 31, 2024. The company has shown significant growth and strategic advancements despite facing some challenges.

Financial Highlights

Beam Global reported revenues of $49.3 million for 2024, a decrease from $67.4 million in 2023. However, this represents a 124% increase over 2022 revenue of $22.0 million. The company achieved a positive gross profit of $7.3 million, or a 15% gross margin, compared to a gross profit of $1.2 million, or a 2% gross margin in 2023. The net loss for 2024 was $11.3 million, an improvement from the net loss of $16.1 million in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

Beam Global has made several strategic moves, including the acquisition of Serbia-based Telcom, which provides in-house production capabilities for power electronics. The company received significant orders from the U.S. Army and the U.S. Department of Homeland Security for its off-grid EV ARC™ systems. Additionally, Beam Global launched four new products and expanded its market presence in the Middle East and Africa through reselling partnerships.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

The company has remained debt-free and has an available $100 million line of credit. Beam Global has also enhanced its leadership team with key hires and announced partnerships with Benzina Zero and Zero Motorcycles. The company expanded its global patent portfolio, securing patents for thermal management technology and wireless/inductive electric vehicle charging powered by renewable energy.

Management's Perspective

Desmond Wheatley, CEO of Beam Global, highlighted the company's tremendous expansion in 2024, including the introduction of new products and geographical expansion. Wheatley emphasized the company's strategic moves to improve gross profitability and set a clear path to being cash-flow positive. He expressed confidence in the company's growth opportunities for 2025 despite political and economic uncertainties in the United States.

Future Outlook

Beam Global's management remains optimistic about the company's growth prospects in 2025, driven by its expanded product portfolio and geographical reach. The company plans to continue investing in sales resources and operational improvements to enhance profitability and market presence.

SEC Filing: Beam Global [ BEEM ] - 8-K - Apr. 11, 2025

data.title: DBV Technologies S.A. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
DBV Technologies S.A., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel therapies for food allergies using its proprietary Viaskin technology platform, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces as it advances its product pipeline, particularly Viaskin Peanut for peanut allergies.

Financial Highlights

  • Operating Income: $4.2 million, a decrease of 74% from the previous year due to lower Research Tax Credit and nil revenue recognition following the termination of the Collaboration Agreement with NESTEC.
  • Net Loss: $113.9 million, an increase of 57% compared to the previous year, primarily due to increased Research and Development expenses.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $1.17, compared to $0.76 in the previous year, reflecting the increased net loss.
  • Total Operating Expenses: $120.7 million, an increase of 31% from the previous year, driven by higher Research and Development expenses and pre-commercialization activities for Viaskin Peanut.

Business Highlights

  • Viaskin Technology Platform: DBV Technologies has developed a novel technology platform called Viaskin, which uses epicutaneous immunotherapy (EPIT) to deliver biologically active compounds through intact skin. This platform is primarily focused on treating food allergies, with a key product candidate being Viaskin Peanut for peanut allergies.
  • Viaskin Peanut Clinical Trials: The company has conducted multiple clinical trials for Viaskin Peanut, including four Phase 2 trials and four completed Phase 3 trials. An ongoing Phase 3 trial is evaluating the product in children aged 4-7, with additional studies planned for toddlers aged 1-3.
  • Regulatory Designations: Viaskin Peanut has received fast track and breakthrough therapy designations from the FDA, which are intended to expedite the review process for treatments addressing serious or life-threatening conditions.
  • Product Pipeline: In addition to Viaskin Peanut, DBV Technologies is developing Viaskin Milk for cow's milk allergy and exploring applications for eosinophilic esophagitis (EoE). The company aims to expand its product pipeline using the Viaskin technology platform.
  • Unmet Medical Need: Peanut allergy is a significant concern, particularly in children, with a prevalence of approximately 2.2% in the pediatric population in the United States. The current standard of care involves strict allergen avoidance and emergency use of epinephrine, highlighting the need for new treatment options.
  • Current and Emerging Treatments: Various treatment approaches for peanut allergy are in development, including oral immunotherapy (OIT), sublingual immunotherapy (SLIT), and EPIT. Viaskin Peanut offers a non-invasive alternative with a potentially favorable safety profile.
  • Viaskin Peanut for Toddlers: The company is advancing Viaskin Peanut for toddlers aged 1-3, with positive results from the EPITOPE Phase 3 trial showing significant treatment effects. A supplemental safety study, COMFORT Toddlers, is planned to support regulatory submissions.
  • Viaskin Peanut for Children 4-7: The VITESSE Phase 3 trial is evaluating a modified Viaskin Peanut patch in children aged 4-7. The trial aims to support a future Biologics License Application (BLA) submission.
  • Manufacturing and Supply: DBV Technologies relies on a proprietary manufacturing process using electrospray technology to produce Viaskin patches. The company has agreements with contract manufacturers for the supply of active pharmaceutical ingredients and finished products.
  • Intellectual Property: The company holds patents related to the Viaskin technology, including the electrostatic patch and allergen desensitization methods. These patents provide protection in major markets such as the United States and Europe.
  • Regulatory Pathway: DBV Technologies is pursuing regulatory approval for Viaskin Peanut in both the United States and Europe. The company has engaged in discussions with the FDA and EMA to align on approval pathways and study requirements.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue developing its Viaskin technology platform, expand its product pipeline, and seek regulatory approvals to bring new treatments to market. The focus remains on addressing unmet medical needs in food allergies and other immunological conditions.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company is advancing its Viaskin technology platform, focusing on the development and potential commercialization of Viaskin Peanut for treating peanut allergies in children. Strategic initiatives include ongoing clinical trials and preparation for regulatory submissions in the U.S. and EU. The company is also exploring additional product candidates and technologies through potential acquisitions or in-licensing agreements.
  • Capital Management: The company has primarily funded its operations through equity financings, including a recent private placement financing (2025 PIPE) that raised $125.5 million. This financing is expected to support operations into June 2026. The company also maintains an At-The-Market (ATM) program to offer and sell up to $100 million of American Depositary Shares. Additionally, the company has no bank debt and relies on equity and public assistance, such as research tax credits, for funding.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates needing additional capital to support the potential launch of Viaskin Peanut and other R&D efforts. It plans to seek financing through public or private equity, debt financings, collaborations, and other non-dilutive options. The company estimates that, with the potential issuance of all warrant shares, it could extend financial visibility into 2028, covering the period through potential commercialization of Viaskin Peanut in the U.S., if approved.

Challenges and Risks

  • Financial Risks: The company faces significant financial risks due to its history of net losses and the expectation of continued losses as it advances its clinical-stage biopharmaceutical products. The company's future financial stability heavily depends on the successful development, regulatory approval, and commercialization of Viaskin Peanut. The company has raised additional funds through a private placement financing, but it still requires substantial additional funding to continue its operations and product development efforts. Failure to secure necessary capital could force the company to delay or terminate its product development efforts.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including potential declines in the market price of its securities, which could result in significant losses for investors. Economic downturns, inflation, and geopolitical events, such as the ongoing conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East, could negatively impact the company's business and financial performance. These macroeconomic conditions may also affect the company's ability to raise additional capital on favorable terms.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks are significant, as the company depends almost entirely on the successful development of its Viaskin technology. The regulatory approval process is complex, lengthy, and uncertain, with the potential for delays or denials from the FDA or other regulatory authorities. The company must demonstrate the safety and efficacy of its product candidates through extensive clinical trials, which are costly and time-consuming. Any setbacks in these trials could jeopardize the company's ability to obtain regulatory approval and successfully market its products.
  • Operational Risks: Operational risks include the company's reliance on third-party manufacturers and suppliers for the production of its product candidates. Any disruptions in the supply chain, such as those caused by natural disasters or public health crises, could adversely affect the company's ability to conduct clinical trials and commercialize its products. Additionally, the company faces challenges in establishing a sales and marketing infrastructure to support the commercialization of its products, if approved.
  • Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the financial challenges posed by the company's ongoing net losses and the need for additional funding. The company plans to address these challenges by seeking additional capital through public or private equity or debt financings, collaborations, and other non-dilutive financings. Management is also focused on advancing the clinical development of Viaskin Peanut and other product candidates to achieve regulatory approval and commercialization.
  • Market Risk Management: The company is exposed to market risks related to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which could adversely affect its financial results. The company operates in multiple countries and is subject to currency exchange rate volatility, which may impact its financial performance and cash flows. Management is monitoring these risks and may implement strategies to mitigate their impact on the company's financial results.

SEC Filing: DBV Technologies S.A. [ DBV ] - 10-K - Apr. 11, 2025

data.title: Western Canada Select heavy crude discount narrows
data.description:
The discount of Western Canada Select (WCS) heavy crude to the North American benchmark West Texas Intermediate futures (WTI) narrowed on Friday, even as the Keystone oil pipeline from Canada to the U.S. remained shut down for a fourth consecutive day.

WCS for May delivery in Hardisty, Alberta, settled at $9.60 a barrel under WTI, according to brokerage CalRock, after having settled at $9.85 under the U.S. benchmark on Thursday.

The Keystone oil pipeline from Canada to the U.S. was shut on Tuesday after an oil spill in North Dakota. South Bow, owner and operator of the 4,327-km (2,689-mile) pipeline, was on Friday still investigating the cause of the leak and had not provided a timeline for the restart.

* The WCS discount widened in the immediate aftermath of the Keystone shutdown, but continues to be historically tight due to U.S. sanctions on heavy crude-producing countries such as Venezuela, as well as lower heavy crude exports from Mexico.

* The differential on Canadian heavy crude also tends to narrow when global oil prices are lower, in part because lower prices mean less competition for pipeline space for Canadian producers.

* Global oil prices saw volatile price swings this week as U.S. President Donald Trump's new tariff regime forced traders to reassess the geopolitical risks facing the crude market.

data.title: Murphy Oil Is Maintained at Sector Perform by Scotiabank
L3Harris's Exposure to Higher Defense Spending Drives UpgradeMarket Talk
data.description: L3Harris could be well positioned in a higher defense spending environment given its exposure to faster-growing parts of the budget, Goldman Sachs analysts say in a research note as they pivot less cautious on the defense sector. The defense contractor doesn't hold any one major platform program that would have risk of spending reduction or margin challenge, the analysts say. Overall, L3Harris's wide margin performance has been better than peers in recent quarters, and it trades at a valuation discount to the average of the large-cap prime contractors, the analysts add. Goldman Sachs raises its recommendation on the L3Harris stock to buy from sell. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

data.title: The Score: CarMax, Harley-Davidson, JPMorgan Chase and More Stocks That Defined the Week
data.description:
By Francesca Fontana

The Score is a weekly review of the biggest stock moves and the news that drove them.

CarMax

CarMax stalled out this week, as President Trump's tariff pause left auto levies in place.

The used-car dealer on Wednesday posted disappointing quarterly earnings and said it could no longer give a time frame for financial goals it has been working toward for years. Despite Trump's walk-back, some levies still stand, such as the 25% tax on automotive imports.

Analysts say the levies will likely drive up the cost of both new and used cars. This could be a mixed bag for CarMax, which could attract more used-car buyers or lose out if shoppers hold off on buying vehicles altogether.

CarMax also said it repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock for $98.5 million last quarter.

CarMax shares plunged 17% Thursday, after rising Wednesday amid a broad-based tariff-pause rally.

Apple

Trump's tariffs cost Apple its crown on Tuesday.

Following Tuesday's closing bell, the iPhone maker ceded its place as the world's most valuable company to Microsoft.

Apple's supply chain is heavily exposed to tariff-related cost increases, as the company sources the iPhone's components from around the world and assembles them primarily in China.

The Wall Street Journal reported Monday that Apple planned to send more iPhones to the U.S. from India to get around the high tariffs on China. On Wednesday, Trump paused some tariffs while raising levies on China. On Thursday, the White House said that the China tariffs add up to 145% — not 125%, as indicated on Wednesday.

Meanwhile, the trade drama has also sparked a sales boost. Fearful of a potential price hike, consumers have scrambled to buy new iPhones and laptops.

Apple shares dropped 5% Tuesday. At Wednesday's close, Apple took back its crown, surpassing Microsoft in market value once more.

Delta Air Lines

Delta predicted 2025 would be its best financial year ever. Trump's tariffs have clouded that view.

The U.S. carrier on Wednesday ditched its full-year financial outlook and said it was too soon to say how air-travel demand would play out.

During an earnings call, Chief Executive Ed Bastian said that the company was working to avoid paying tariffs on the dozens of planes it is set to receive from Airbus this year.

Trump on Wednesday afternoon announced a 90-day pause on certain tariffs to most countries. The pause sent stocks soaring Wednesday, lifting the airlines from its recent doldrums.

Delta's shares rose 23% Wednesday. United Airlines rose 26%, and American Airlines rose 23%.

Walmart

Trump's tariff-pause made a splash as it interrupted Walmart's two-day investor meeting.

Earlier Wednesday the big-box retailer's shares were rising against a market slide, after the company said it would maintain its current quarter's financial forecast.

During the event, executives said the trade turbulence was an opportunity to grab market share as cash-strapped shoppers looked for lower prices. So far, Walmart wasn't canceling purchase orders of goods from China, Mexico and other countries, which account for about a third of U.S. sales.

By early afternoon, Trump had changed course, announcing a 90-day pause on many of those levies. A public-relations executive told reporters Walmart's priorities remained the same as before the about-face: managing inventory, keeping prices low and managing costs.

Walmart shares ended 9.6% higher Wednesday.

Harley-Davidson

Harley-Davidson is looking for a new leader to steer it through tariff chaos and slumping sales.

The motorcycle maker said Tuesday that Chief Executive Jochen Zeitz plans to retire after five years on the job. Zeitz will remain in his position until a successor is chosen.

During his tenure, Zeitz has boosted Harley's profit but has seen sales of the bikes continue to decline. The company last year sold 151,000 motorcycles worldwide, less than half as many as it sold in 2008.

Harley's prospects have also been shaken in the trade war launched by the Trump administration, with the European Union threatening to impose 50% tariffs on the company's bikes.

Harley shares declined 8.6% Tuesday.

Amazon.com

Tech giants are sticking to their AI spending plans, despite recession risks.

In his annual shareholder letter Thursday, Amazon Chief Executive Andy Jassy said that he believed AI was a "once-in-a-lifetime reinvention of everything we know," and that customers, shareholders and the business "will be well-served by our investing aggressively now."

That followed a keynote speech from Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai, in which he reiterated a plan to spend about $75 billion on AI this fiscal year.

Together, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Alphabet and Amazon plan more than $270 billion of capital spending on data centers this year, according to a Citigroup estimate.

After rallying 12% on Wednesday, Amazon shares ended 5.2% lower Thursday.

JPMorgan Chase

Wall Street celebrated big banks' latest earnings and digested executives' warnings amid trade turbulence.

JPMorgan Chase's first-quarter earnings and revenue rose, but the bank raised concerns that a slowing global economy could cause loan losses and paralysis in the capital markets.

Chief Executive Jamie Dimon warned that the economy "is facing considerable turbulence." Even accounting for Trump's tariff pause announced Wednesday, the bank's economists see a likely recession on the horizon this year.

Wells Fargo and Morgan Stanley also posted higher profits for the first quarter that topped expectations, but executives Friday echoed Dimon's concern about the future.

JPMorgan shares rose 4% Friday.

Our weekly markets news roundup is now part of the WSJ's What's News podcast. Host Francesca Fontana discusses the biggest stock moves of the week and the news that drove them. Check out What's News in Markets at wsj.com/podcasts or wherever you listen.

Write to Francesca Fontana at francesca.fontana@wsj.com.

data.title: Dollar Loses 0.59% to 20.3124 Mexican PesosData Talk
data.description:
Dollar/Mexican peso: 20.3124 Mexican pesos per dollar (0.0492 dollar per Mexican peso)

  • This week the dollar lost 0.59% vs. the Mexican peso
  • Largest one-week percentage decline since Friday, March 14, 2025
  • Snaps a three-week winning streak
  • Today the dollar lost 0.73% vs. the Mexican peso
  • Down two of the past three sessions
  • Off 2.57% from its 52-week high of 20.8489 hit Thursday, Jan. 16, 2025
  • Up 22.66% from its 52-week low of 16.5597 hit Monday, May 20, 2024
  • Rose 22.06% vs the Mexican peso from 52 weeks ago
  • Month-to-date it is down 0.77% vs the Mexican peso
  • Year-to-date the dollar is down 2.48% vs the Mexican peso

Data based on 5 p.m. ET values

Source: Tullett Prebon and Dow Jones Market Data

data.title: Grades little changed even as U.S. energy firms cut oil rigs
data.description:
Grades were steady on Friday even as U.S. energy firms this week cut oil rigs by the most in a week since June 2023.

WTI Midland was unchanged, while Mars edged 10 cents higher.

Baker Hughes said oil rigs fell by nine to 480 this week. Oil producers were starting to think about cutting output and jobs due to a double whammy of higher crude output from OPEC and on-again, off-again tariffs that have dented demand, sources and industry executives said.

Meanwhile, China announced on Friday it will impose a 125% tariff on U.S. goods starting on Saturday, up from the previously announced 84%, after Trump raised tariffs against China to effectively 145% on Thursday.

At least two vessels carrying Venezuelan crude chartered by Chevron are now stalled in Venezuelan waters due to state oil company PDVSA canceling their export authorizations after the U.S. imposed secondary tariffs on the OPEC member's buyers, three sources with knowledge of the matter said on Friday.

* Light Louisiana Sweet (WTC-LLS) for May delivery eased 10 cents to a midpoint of a $2.20 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $2.00 and $2.40 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* Mars Sour (WTC-MRS) strengthened 10 cents to a midpoint of a $1.1 premium and was seen bid and offered between a 90-cent and $1.3 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* WTI Midland (WTC-WTM) was unchanged at a midpoint of a 90-cent premium and was seen bid and offered between a 70-cent and $1.10 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* West Texas Sour (WTC-WTS) strengthened 15 cents to a midpoint of a 30-cent premium and was seen bid and offered between a 15-cent and 45-cent a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* WTI at East Houston (WTC-MEH), also known as MEH, traded between a $1.00 and $1.50 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* ICE Brent June futures rose $1.43 to settle at $64.76 a barrel on Friday.

* WTI May crude futures rose $1.43 to settle at $61.5 a barrel on Friday.

* The Brent/WTI spread (WTCLc1-LCOc1) widened 10 cents to last trade at minus $3.80, after hitting a high of minus $3.72 and a low of minus $3.88.

data.title: Communication Services Shares Rise FridayCommunication Services Roundup
data.description:
Communication services stocks rose Friday, ending a volatile week with gains as investors processed the impact of Trump's tariffs.

Needham analyst Laura Martin reduced her price targets on 16 digital media stocks, citing the negative economic climate and likely recession. Analysts are focused on Netflix ahead of the company's first-quarter earnings report next Thursday.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

data.title: Utility Shares End Choppy Week With GainsUtilities Roundup
data.description:
Utility stocks rose Friday, ending a volatile week with gains as investors processed the impact of Trump's tariffs.

The sector is seen as a safe bet during the stock market's current decline, due to consistent demand for electricity.

TD Cowen maintains below-consensus revenue estimates for First Solar due to cautious project developers and warranty issues with some products.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

data.title: Technology Shares Rise Amid Tariff FightTech Roundup
data.description:
Technology stocks rose Friday, ending a volatile week with gains as investors processed the impact of Trump's tariffs.

New rules from China targeting U.S. semiconductor companies that manufacture chips in the U.S. hit some big names in the sector, such as Intel and Texas Instruments.

While raw semiconductors have been exempt from President Trump's tariffs on China, China has its own levies against the U.S. The new rules stipulate that the "origin point" of a chip will be the location of the fabrication plant, not where it gets packaged, Bernstein Research analysts noted.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

data.title: Financial Shares Kick Off Earnings SeasonFinancials Roundup
data.description:
Financial shares rose along with a broader rally in U.S. equity markets as March inflation indicators cooled and banks kicked off the quarterly earnings season.

The head of the biggest bank in the U.S. and other financial heavyweights warned of tariff-related economic turbulence ahead.

JPMorgan Chase reported a 9% jump in earnings for the first quarter. Chief Executive Jamie Dimon said on a call following the results that corporate confidence is flagging and that recession risks are high in light of the erratic tariff campaign being waged by the U.S.

BlackRock's quarterly net income fell slightly from a year earlier, while the investment firm's assets under management edged higher to a record $11.6 trillion.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com
